description_long: This is a heavy-duty pipe wrench with a robust, dark metallic head and a thick, cylindrical wooden handle. The head features an adjustable jaw mechanism with visible teeth for gripping pipes, and a knurled adjustment nut for changing the jaw width. The handle is smooth, rounded, and appears to be made of polished wood, providing a comfortable grip. The metal head shows a rugged, industrial design with a matte finish and some minor surface irregularities. The overall construction suggests durability and suitability for tough urban repair or plumbing tasks. No visible branding or decorative elements are present, emphasizing its utilitarian function.
description: A sturdy pipe wrench with a dark metal adjustable head and a smooth, rounded wooden handle, designed for gripping and turning pipes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the jaw opening and adjustment nut, with the handle extending backward.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the full length of the wrench, the teeth of the jaw, and the wooden handle.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the end of the wooden handle and the back of the adjustment mechanism.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile displaying the jaw, adjustment nut, and the handle's length.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle, cylindrical, smooth finish, no visible grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: adjustable pipe wrench head, steel, with serrated jaws and a knurled adjustment nut
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: minor surface irregularities and slight discoloration on the metal head, no major damage visible
